analysis_result: HALLMARK_WNT_BETA_CATENIN G1 vs G2: NES=-0.942 FDR=0.845. Did not reproduce. Paper used different gene set (likely WNT_UP.V1_UP or similar C2 set).
discrepancy_analysis:
A. REPORTED vs MODEL
- Reported (Fig 3d, MPNST-G1 WNT Pathway panel): NES = 1.2238, FDR = 0.3192 (positive enrichment trend in G1, non-significant).
- Model: NES = -0.942, FDR = 0.845 for HALLMARK_WNT_BETA_CATENIN_SIGNALING (G1 vs G2).
- Signs are opposite and magnitudes differ substantially; the FDRs are on different sides of nominal significance thresholds in the data-visible sense. Relative difference on NES is well beyond ±10%; this is clearly DISCREPANT.

B. MAGNITUDE
- NES sign flip plus a ~|1.2 - (-0.9)| / |1.2| > 150% relative delta on NES. Classified MAJOR (>20%).

C. WHAT THE PAPER ACTUALLY DID
- Methods (RNA sequencing and analysis): 'Pathway analysis was performed on the DEG from the indicated pairwise analysis using Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) package (Broad Institute).' The paper does not name a specific MSigDB collection in the Methods.
- Fig 3c (Top 10 upregulated pathways in MPNST-G1 and MPNST-G2) lists gene-set names that are unambiguously MSigDB C6 (oncogenic signatures v1) identifiers: ATF2_UP.V1_UP, RB_DN.V1_UP, GCNP_SHH_UP_EARLY.V1_UP, GCNP_SHH_UP_LATE.V1_UP, MYC_UP.V1_UP, E2F1_UP.V1_UP, MTOR_UP.V1_UP, RB_P130_DN.V1_UP, CSR_LATE_UP.V1_UP, RB_P107_DN.V1_UP (MPNST-G1) and RB_P107_DN.V1_UP, BMI1_DN.V1_UP, CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP, CYCLIN_D1_KE.V1_UP, RB_P130_DN.V1_UP, MYC_UP.V1_UP, VEGF_A_UP.V1_UP, SRC_UP.V1_UP, BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP, WNT_UP.V1_UP (MPNST-G2).
- The Fig 3d 'WNT Pathway' enrichment plots are therefore from the same C6 run; the gene set shown is almost certainly WNT_UP.V1_UP (a C6 oncogenic signature of WNT-pathway-responsive genes from Bild et al.) and/or BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP (beta-catenin signature), not the Hallmark WNT/beta-catenin set.

D. WHAT THE MODEL DID
- Model queried HALLMARK_WNT_BETA_CATENIN_SIGNALING (MSigDB H collection). This gene set has only ~42 genes focused on canonical beta-catenin core components and is known to be underpowered / often non-significant in bulk transcriptome GSEA where a broader WNT-responsive transcriptional program is the real signal.
- Different gene-set membership -> different leading edge -> different NES and FDR. Sign flip is unsurprising: Hallmark WNT/beta-catenin contains several Notch/beta-catenin-ambiguous and developmental genes whose expression direction in MPNST-G1 vs G2 is not the same as an oncogenic WNT-response signature.

E. ROOT CAUSE
- Classification 2:VARIABLE_CHOICE -- specifically, the wrong MSigDB gene set was used. The paper's Fig 3d WNT panel is a C6 oncogenic signature (WNT_UP.V1_UP, possibly alongside BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP), not HALLMARK_WNT_BETA_CATENIN_SIGNALING.

F. PROPOSED FIX
- Re-run fgsea on the same MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2 edgeR-ranked stat using the C6 collection (msigdbr: category = 'C6') and report NES/FDR for WNT_UP.V1_UP (and for completeness BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP). The existing recipe (recipes/R/fgsea_c6.R) already defaults to C6 and is the correct tool for this reproduction. Expected: WNT_UP.V1_UP should show the same positive-in-G1 direction the paper reports with NES around +1.2 and an FDR in the 0.2-0.4 range consistent with the figure.

G. CONFIDENCE
- HIGH that the root cause is gene-set choice (C6 vs H). The Fig 3c gene-set names are unmistakably C6 identifiers, and Fig 3d is drawn from the same GSEA run. The paper's own text even flags WNT_UP.V1_UP as enriched in MPNST-G2 (panel 3c lower).
proposed_fix: Re-run fgsea with MSigDB C6 (oncogenic signatures) collection rather than Hallmark, on the edgeR QLF-ranked MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2 gene list, and report NES/FDR for WNT_UP.V1_UP (the C6 WNT oncogenic signature). The existing recipes/R/fgsea_c6.R defaults to C6 and is the correct tool; Fig 3c gene-set names (e.g., WNT_UP.V1_UP, BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP, MYC_UP.V1_UP, RB_DN.V1_UP) unambiguously identify the collection used in Fig 3d.
